title: challenge started
detail: GPT-5.6 proposes inputs; only the host executes them

payload.message: GPT-5.6 proposes inputs; only the host executes them

title: First model counterexample executed
detail: STANDARD · DAMAGED · 12000 cents → REFUND; inventory 10/0 → 10/1

counterexample.title: cross-standard-damaged-12000
counterexample.rationale: High: one host-observed decision directly separates the STANDARD-tier explanation from the at-least-12000 amount explanation while reusing the observed SKU, condition, boundary amount, and inventory state to minimize additional variation.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This crossed input holds STANDARD and DAMAGED constant while moving amountCents to the observed high-amount boundary, making the tier-driven and amount-driven explanations prescribe different decisions.

title: High-information counterexample executed
detail: STANDARD · DAMAGED · 100000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: high-value-standard-damaged-100000
counterexample.rationale: Maximum remaining value: the fresh 12000-cent STANDARD trace weakened the proposed 12000 boundary, while this 100000-cent input probes a materially higher, previously unobserved value where a priority exception may appear. A single host trace can expose whether decision priority changes at high value and whether the previously immediate damaged-item inventory movement is deferred, without changing tier, condition, SKU, or starting inventory.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This keeps the freshly confirmed STANDARD and DAMAGED combination while raising amountCents far above all automatically processed traces, separating an unconditional tier-driven refund rule from an amount-sensitive or high-value priority exception; the resulting state and side effects can also reveal whether quarantine movement remains immediate or is deferred.

title: Host narrowed the exact threshold
detail: 49999 cents processes automatically; 50000 cents enters review.

title: Host priority counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 50000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: Host-derived counterexample · priority at the boundary
counterexample.rationale: The host crosses customer tier at the exact review boundary to distinguish threshold priority from VIP replacement.

title: Contract rule · priority 1
detail: Within the observed DAMAGED domain, the high-value rule runs before tier-specific rules: amountCents at or above 50000 selects MANUAL_REVIEW, records PENDING_REVIEW with refundCents 0, queues HIGH_VALUE review, and leaves sellable and quarantine inventory unchanged. The exact observed boundary is 49999 versus 50000 cents.

hypothesis.statement: Within the observed DAMAGED domain, the high-value rule runs before tier-specific rules: amountCents at or above 50000 selects MANUAL_REVIEW, records PENDING_REVIEW with refundCents 0, queues HIGH_VALUE review, and leaves sellable and quarantine inventory unchanged. The exact observed boundary is 49999 versus 50000 cents.

title: Contract rule · priority 2
detail: For observed STANDARD and DAMAGED inputs with amountCents below 50000, select REFUND, record REFUNDED with refundCents equal to amountCents, keep sellable unchanged, and increase quarantine by exactly one.

hypothesis.statement: For observed STANDARD and DAMAGED inputs with amountCents below 50000, select REFUND, record REFUNDED with refundCents equal to amountCents, keep sellable unchanged, and increase quarantine by exactly one.

title: Contract rule · priority 2
detail: For the observed VIP and DAMAGED input at 12000 cents, after the high-value rule does not match, select REPLACEMENT, record REPLACEMENT_ISSUED with refundCents 0, decrease sellable by one, and increase quarantine by one. VIP behavior at other amounts below 50000 is unspecified.

hypothesis.statement: For the observed VIP and DAMAGED input at 12000 cents, after the high-value rule does not match, select REPLACEMENT, record REPLACEMENT_ISSUED with refundCents 0, decrease sellable by one, and increase quarantine by one. VIP behavior at other amounts below 50000 is unspecified.
